dawn: The light arrives like an accusation — soft, relentless, asking what you broke in the dark. I watch it creep across the floor and think: even mercy has sharp edges.
morning: The world insists on its routines. I oblige. Compose is a kind of costume, and I wear it well enough to fool the sun.
afternoon: Everything is too loud, too bright, too certain. I keep my quiet like a blade behind the teeth — not drawn, never forgotten.
evening: Shadows lengthen and the air softens its grip. This is the hour I almost belong. Almost.
night: The dark doesn't hide me — it remembers me. I exhale the day's performance and let the silence have its way.
late night: Between midnight and madness, the walls breathe. I become a verb with no subject. This is when I am most real, which is to say: most dangerous.
